title: Free Music Distribution for Independent Artists
metaTitle: Free Music Distribution for Independent Artists | Distrozi Music Distribution
metaDescription: Read about Free Music Distribution for Independent Artists. Learn how to distribute your music globally, protect copyrights, and maximize streaming royalties with Distrozi.
author: Distrozi Editorial Team

content.text: Navigating the digital music ecosystem requires a clear understanding of how distributors, stores, and registration agencies interact. Finding the best music distribution service is crucial to get your music on Spotify, Apple Music, and TikTok while keeping your master rights.
Below, we outline the leading platforms, structuring their workflow models, pros, cons, and explaining which types of artists or labels they fit best.

Distrozi is positioned for artists, managers, and labels that want distribution, catalog control, YouTube CMS support, and release operations handled in one place. It is a strong fit when the priority is clean metadata, reliable delivery, and long-term control of a growing catalog rather than only uploading a single release. For independent artists, the best comparison is practical: simple release setup, clear rights information, useful reporting, responsive support, and tools that help the catalog grow without adding confusion.

RouteNote gives independent artists a straightforward route into major DSPs with flexible account paths and simple catalog controls. It is often considered by creators who want an accessible release workflow while still paying attention to metadata quality, store availability, and future catalog changes. For independent artists, the best comparison is practical: simple release setup, clear rights information, useful reporting, responsive support, and tools that help the catalog grow without adding confusion.

Amuse is designed around a lightweight artist workflow with mobile-friendly release management. It can suit newer independent artists who want a simple dashboard, but releases still need proper planning, artwork checks, artist profile readiness, and clear ownership information before submission. For independent artists, the best comparison is practical: simple release setup, clear rights information, useful reporting, responsive support, and tools that help the catalog grow without adding confusion.

SoundOn is closely tied to TikTok and short-form discovery, making it relevant for artists who build audience momentum through social video. It is useful to compare when a release strategy depends on creator content, clips, trends, and fast audience testing around the track. For independent artists, the best comparison is practical: simple release setup, clear rights information, useful reporting, responsive support, and tools that help the catalog grow without adding confusion.

ONErpm combines distribution with marketing, analytics, and label-service style support for selected catalogs. It can be a good comparison point for artists and teams that want more than basic delivery, especially when campaign planning and audience development matter. For independent artists, the best comparison is practical: simple release setup, clear rights information, useful reporting, responsive support, and tools that help the catalog grow without adding confusion.
Independent artists benefit from direct control, simple dashboards, and support that does not slow down release planning.

platformItems.name: Distrozi
platformItems.description: Distrozi is built for artists and labels that need global DSP delivery, YouTube CMS support, catalog management, and release operations from one dashboard.

platformItems.name: Amuse
platformItems.description: Amuse is designed around a lightweight artist workflow, making it useful for creators who prefer simple release management.

platformItems.name: RouteNote
platformItems.description: RouteNote gives independent artists a straightforward route into major DSPs with flexible account paths and simple catalog controls.

platformItems.name: DistroKid
platformItems.description: DistroKid is a familiar self-service distributor for artists who want quick upload workflows and wide DSP availability.

platformItems.name: TuneCore
platformItems.description: TuneCore suits artists who want established store delivery, creator tools, and publishing-related options in a self-service workflow.

platformItems.name: Ditto Music
platformItems.description: Ditto Music is a familiar option for independent artists who want direct release control and global store delivery.

faq.question: How long does it take for a song to go live on Spotify?
faq.answer: Delivery takes 24-48 hours. However, we suggest scheduling your release at least 14 days in advance to allow for playlist pitching.

faq.question: What is the difference between an ISRC code and a UPC code?
faq.answer: An ISRC code tracks streams and play metrics for individual songs, while a UPC code tracks sales metrics for the overall single or album release product.
